title: Studi'OU

MENU

Giriş

Introduction

Studi’OU, the academic journal of the Faculty of Art, Design and Architecture at Istanbul Okan University, is now accepting submissions for its third issue, which will be published in March 2026.

The journal seeks to advance scholarly knowledge by publishing original research articles, critical essays, and theoretical discussions in the fields of art, design, architecture, and art history. Studi’OU emphasizes not only the study of individual artistic and design practices but also their broader social, cultural, and historical contexts, offering a critical platform for interdisciplinary dialogue. By fostering a connection between theoretical frameworks and applied practices, the journal welcomes contributions that explore the intersections of creative production, cultural theory, and societal transformation.

Ana Başlıklar

Disiplinlerarası Kesitler

Key Areas

Art History, Aesthetics, Art Theory

Architecture, Urban and Interior Design

Graphic Design and Visual Communication Design

Cinema and Media Studies

Fashion and Textile Design

Cultural Heritage, Conservation, Curatorial Studies

Interdisciplinary Perspectives

Publication Policy & Ethics

All submissions undergo a double-blind peer review process.

The similarity index must be below 15%.

Studies requiring ethics approval must include details of approval.

Authors must declare potential conflicts of interest.

Submissions must follow APA 7 citation and referencing style.

Abstracts in both Turkish and English (max. 300 words) and 3–5 keywords are mandatory.

15 Ekim 2025 / October 15, 2025

Gönderim Koşulları

Makaleler,

studiou@okan.edu.tr

Submission Guidelines

Manuscripts must be submitted to

studiou@okan.edu.tr

no later than Wednesday, December 17, 2025, at 23:59, in accordance with the author guidelines specified on the

official website of Studi’OU

. Each author is required to provide their ORCID number, academic title, institutional affiliation, and contact information. In addition, the article submission and copyright transfer; author contribution; publication ethics and originality (plagiarism) forms available on the official website of Studi’OU must be included in the application file. The file format should be either “.docx” or “.doc.” Images should be submitted as separate files at 300 dpi resolution.

Authors must adhere to the important dates specified in the call for papers. Submissions with missing documents and/or incorrect formatting will not be considered for evaluation.
